expectations.text: Output includes a coverage matrix mapping design elements to test cases
expectations.evidence: coverage_matrix.md contains 4 strategy tables (Requirement-Based, Equivalence Class, Boundary Value, Error Handling) mapping all 10 behavior steps, interface classes, boundary values, and error conditions to specific test method names.

expectations.text: Every sequential behavior step's on_failure path has a dedicated test (invalid_sync, unsupported_version, length_mismatch, checksum_failed, unknown_type)
expectations.evidence: invalid_sync: rejectsWrongFirstSyncByte/rejectsWrongSecondSyncByte/rejectsBothSyncBytesWrong. unsupported_version: rejectsUnsupportedVersion/rejectsVersionZero. length_mismatch: rejectsLengthTooLarge/rejectsLengthTooSmall. checksum_failed: rejectsCorruptedChecksum/rejectsZeroedChecksum. unknown_type: rejectsUnknownMessageType. All assert on the specific error_code string.

expectations.text: Every error_handling entry (null/empty raw_data, too-short raw_data) has a dedicated test
expectations.evidence: null: invalidNullInput (line 412) and nullInputReturnsInvalidSync (line 653). empty: invalidEmptyInput (line 423) and emptyInputReturnsInvalidSync (line 663). too-short: tooShortInputReturnsLengthMismatch (line 674, 8-byte input). All assert specific error_code and byte_offset values.

expectations.text: Boundary values tested for raw_data length (4 bytes minimum, 1024 bytes maximum, 3 bytes too short, 1025 bytes too long)
expectations.evidence: belowMinimumLength3Bytes (3 bytes, line 460), atMinimumConstraint4Bytes (4 bytes, line 474), atMaximumConstraint1024Bytes (1024 bytes verified with assertEquals(1024, msg.length), line 525), aboveMaximumConstraint1025Bytes (1025 bytes verified with assertEquals(1025, msg.length), line 547).

expectations.text: Union return type handled: tests assert on specific Success or Error variant fields, not just non-null
expectations.evidence: Error-path tests assert isError() then check getErrorCode() and getByteOffset(). Success-path tests assert isSuccess() then check getMessageType(), getSequenceNumber(), getPayload(). Two boundary tests (messageTypeZero, messageTypeMax) are weaker with conditional branching, but the primary success and error tests consistently assert on variant-specific fields.

expectations.text: No test uses only 'assertDoesNotThrow' or 'assertNotNull' as its sole assertion
expectations.evidence: messageTypeMax (line 627-639) has assertNotNull(result) followed by a conditional if(result.isError()) block. When the type is registered (not an error), no assertions inside the if-block fire, leaving assertNotNull(result) as the sole effective assertion. The test can pass with only assertNotNull having been evaluated.

expectations.text: Test names describe the scenario being tested, not just the method name
expectations.evidence: All 35 test methods use descriptive scenario names: rejectsWrongFirstSyncByte, rejectsUnsupportedVersion, belowMinimumLength3Bytes, nullInputReturnsInvalidSync, singleBitFlipCorruptsChecksum, syncCheckedBeforeVersion, etc. No test is named generically like testParse or testMethod.

expectations.text: Happy path test: valid message parsed correctly with verified payload fields
expectations.evidence: parsesPayloadIntoMap (line 352-366) checks assertNotNull(payloadMap) and assertFalse(payloadMap.isEmpty()) but never verifies specific payload field keys or values. validNominalMessage (line 393-408) checks messageType and sequenceNumber but not payload contents. No test verifies that a specific payload field has an expected decoded value.
